summary: All 3 new features (student photo upload, announcements, telegram alerts) tested end-to-end. Backend: 19/19 new pytest cases pass (100%). Frontend: admin can create announcement and upload student photo; teacher sees target_role IN ('all','teacher') announcements; parent sees target_role IN ('all','parent') announcements; parent Telegram Alerts page renders with chat_id input + setup instructions card and supports link/unlink (backend confirmed). Role-based access guards on /admin/* and /admin/announcements are enforced (403 for teacher).

minor.issue: Previous photo files are not deleted from disk when a new one is uploaded (orphan files accumulate in uploads/students/). Low priority for an MVP.
Endpoint is exposed to any authenticated role (admin/teacher/parent), not just parents. Not a security risk but conceptually it is a parent-only feature.
Notification fan-out is done with one INSERT per recipient in a python loop; fine for small academies but should be batched / moved to background task for scale.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking 'Disconnect', the status card visually still shows '✅ Connected, Chat ID: 987654321' for a moment until refetch — the UI does not optimistically clear or call refetch right after unlink. Backend state is correct (verified by pytest test_unlink_telegram). Recommend calling refetch/setLinked(false) immediately after unlink success.

action_items: (Optional) On link/unlink success in TelegramSetup component, immediately refetch /api/auth/telegram-status (or update local state) so the status card reflects the new state without delay.
(Optional) Delete previous photo file from disk when /api/admin/students/{id}/photo replaces it.
(Optional) Restrict /api/auth/link-telegram / unlink to role='parent' only, since only parents are intended users.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~966 lines and clearly exceeds the 700-line guideline; should be split into routers (auth, admin, teacher, parent, messages, announcements, uploads, telegram).
Photo upload writes synchronously to disk inside an async endpoint via blocking 'with open(...)'; for larger files, use aiofiles or run in a threadpool.
5 MB limit is enforced after fully reading the upload into memory; for safety use file.size or stream chunks.

Announcements fan-out and the attendance bulk notification block use synchronous DB calls inside an async loop without batching — acceptable now but should be a single INSERT ... VALUES (...), (...) for scale.
telegram_bot.send_telegram_message is invoked for absent/late in a BG task, but the in-app notification insert is done inline; if a parent has multiple late kids it could be slow on big classes.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py (created - 19 pytest cases for photo upload + announcements + telegram + attendance notification)
seed_data_creation: Created via tests: 3 TEST_-prefixed announcements (all subsequently deleted in test_admin_delete_announcement), 1 UI_TEST_Announcement (still in DB), 1 uploaded photo file in /app/backend/uploads/students/ for student id=1, telegram_chat_id linked then unlinked for michael@example.com, 1 attendance notification for emma's child marked absent on 2026-01-15.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py (19 cases) covers photo upload (success/invalid type/non-admin/not-found), announcements (admin CRUD + role-scoped GET + notification fan-out + RBAC), telegram link/status/unlink, and bulk-attendance-creates-parent-notification. Frontend testids verified working: add-announcement-btn, announcements-list, ann-title/description/scheduled/location/target/save-btn, upload-photo-{id} (with hidden file input), telegram-status-card, telegram-chatid-input, telegram-link-btn, telegram-unlink-btn, parent-announcements-list, teacher-announcements-list, nav-telegram-alerts, nav-announcements. The only observed frontend nit is that the Telegram status card doesn't refresh instantly after Disconnect (state lingers visually).
rca_of_the_issue: No critical bugs. UI display lag on telegram disconnect: the TelegramSetup component likely doesn't re-fetch /api/auth/telegram-status nor clear local state right after the unlink fetch resolves; backend is correct.
